title: Disable automatic update after automation

posts.author: Mayon85 (Marion)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

When an email is sent from an automation (eg: when item is created, send an email to…), an automatic update is created on the item. I would like to disable that update.
I found this post wich deals with the same problem : https://community.monday.com/t/turn-off-automatic-update-creation-with-gmail-integration/
I’m creating a new post to use the category “Feature requests”

In my case, my automation sends an email to all board subscribers. So far so good. However, an update is created for each email sent. So I can easily have 5, 10 or even 15, unnecessary updates…
Things get complicated when I want to move my element to another board. I have to delete all the updates so as not to pollute the new board in which the element is newley located.

And to top it all off, I’m the owner of the automation, so I’m the only one who can delete the updates! Nonsense… please let us disable the automatic updates!

posts.author: Bkostinsky (Brandon)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this? We need this bad. Has been requested for awhile by many users.

posts.author: osmire (Oscar)
posts.cookedHtml: Does anyone know hot to solve this? Having the same issue here…
